summary: Mighty Quinn breakout, high‑energy second set with Crazy Fingers
blurb: The second set shines with a blistering rendition of The Mighty Quinn followed by Samson & Delilah, He's Gone and an epic Crazy Fingers. Jerry’s guitar is on fire and Brent adds vocal depth, while Mickey and Billy drive the jams. The first set is uneven but still offers solid moments.
review: On December 30, 1985 at the Oakland‑Alameda County Coliseum the Grateful Dead delivered a show that lives up to the reputation of a quintessential ’85 concert. While the first set meanders through a mix of bluesy covers and familiar rockers – with Gimme Some Lovin’ and Stagger Lee starting strong but Looks Like Rain falling flat – the real magic erupts in the second half. The Mighty Quinn (Quinn The Eskimo) launches an unstoppable momentum that carries through Samson & Delilah, He's Gone, I Need A Miracle and a soaring Crazy Fingers. Jerry Garcia shreds on Samson, weaves tender motifs on He's Gone, and takes the band into a spacey solo during Crazy Fingers, only to stumble briefly on vocals before Brent Mydland lifts the vibe with his harmonies. The rhythm section—Mickey’s Egyptian‑flavored flair on Crazy Fingers and Billy’s driving pulse—keeps the energy high through Space > The Other One, Wharf Rat and a jubilant Sugar Magnolia finale. For Deadheads seeking the definitive listening experience, the Miller soundboard (gd1985-12-30.sbd.miller.77405.sbeok.flac16) captures the crispness and separation of the band while preserving the live spark that makes this night memorable.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All five recordings are uniformly positive, repeatedly praising the second‑set surge (Mighty Quinn, Crazy Fingers) and Jerry’s stellar guitar work. Multiple sources highlight the same standout songs and tight band interplay, creating a strong consensus of excellence.

best_recording.reason: Crisp soundboard with clear instrument separation, captures the standout second‑set energy and all highlighted songs without noticeable drop‑outs.

key_highlights: Mighty Quinn (Quinn The Eskimo) breakout performance
Epic Crazy Fingers jam with Egyptian‑flavored drum pattern
Jerry’s shredding on Samson & Delilah and tender solo on He's Gone
Space > The Other One segues into a soaring second set

band_performance.Jerry: Shredding on Samson, flawless tone on Quinn, tender motifs in He's Gone, space‑y solo during Crazy Fingers; confident lead guitar throughout with a brief vocal slip.
band_performance.Phil: Solid bass foundation despite the first‑set looseness; keeps the groove steady through the high‑energy second set.
band_performance.Bob: Vocals energized by an echo effect on the second set, especially effective on the Mighty Quinn and Crazy Fingers sections.
band_performance.Brent: Intense keyboard interplay and crucial vocal harmonies that lift the jam after Jerry’s vocal slip; adds depth throughout the second half.
band_performance.Mickey: Sprightly, almost Egyptian‑flavored rhythm during Crazy Fingers, driving the band forward in the second set.
band_performance.Billy: Driving drum power propels the jams, especially evident in the Space > The Other One transition and the final Wharf Rat/Sugar Magnolia climax.
